Honden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara|Q641491}}{{jalink|Kakami Ogase-chō|Q131912300}}3-46-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}
{{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578}}
{{jalink|Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{jalink|7th century|Q8089}} | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kakamigahara|Q641491}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|shikinaisha}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{jalink|Kakami Village|Q11412364|Kakami district}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{jalink|Kakamigōri|Q11412369}}, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{jalink|Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264}}, and is now an {{jalink|otabisho|Q11489117}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{jalink|Murakuniza|Q11523051}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{jalink|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{jalink|Asuka period|Q210688}} by the {{jalink|Murakuni clan|Q11523054}} with {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} and {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{jalink|Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052}} jointly enshrined {{jalink|Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264}}. Around {{jalink|900|Q23460}} ({{jalink|Shōhō|Q1193592}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{jalink|1877|Q7773}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 10), {{jalink|Murakuniza|Q11523051}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{jalink|kabuki|Q199701}}), and in {{jalink|1882|Q7812}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{jalink|1973|Q2477}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} * {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578}} * {{jalink|Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{jalink|jikai kabuki}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{jalink|Murakuniza|Q11523051}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/appeal/bunkazai/000754.html |title=City-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kakamigahara City |accessdate=2013-04-25}} :* Muku tree at Murakuni Shrine otabisho : === Reference images === 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-村国神社と村国座murakuniza006.jpg|Murakuni Shrine and Murakuniza 村国神社 {{commons category|Category:Murakuni-jinja}} [[JA:村国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523055}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Murakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza028.jpg|270px]]
Honden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara|Q641491}}{{jalink|Kakami Ogase-chō|Q131912300}}3-46-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}
{{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578}}
{{jalink|Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{jalink|7th century|Q8089}} | 本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kakamigahara|Q641491}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|shikinaisha}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{jalink|Kakami Village|Q11412364|Kakami district}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{jalink|Kakamigōri|Q11412369}}, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{jalink|Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264}}, and is now an {{jalink|otabisho|Q11489117}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{jalink|Murakuniza|Q11523051}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{jalink|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{jalink|Asuka period|Q210688}} by the {{jalink|Murakuni clan|Q11523054}} with {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} and {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{jalink|Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052}} jointly enshrined {{jalink|Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264}}. Around {{jalink|900|Q23460}} ({{jalink|Shōhō|Q1193592}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{jalink|1877|Q7773}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 10), {{jalink|Murakuniza|Q11523051}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{jalink|kabuki|Q199701}}), and in {{jalink|1882|Q7812}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{jalink|1973|Q2477}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} * {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578}} * {{jalink|Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{jalink|jikai kabuki}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{jalink|Murakuniza|Q11523051}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/appeal/bunkazai/000754.html |title=City-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kakamigahara City |accessdate=2013-04-25}} :* Muku tree at Murakuni Shrine otabisho : === Reference images === 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-村国神社と村国座murakuniza006.jpg|Murakuni Shrine and Murakuniza 村国神社 about 30 minutes on foot. * {{jalink|Kakamigahara Fureai Bus|Q11412316}} Inaba Line, get off at "{{jalink|Kakamigahara Municipal Kakami Elementary School|Q11412331|Kakami-shō Nishi}}" bus stop, about According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill||lt=jikai kabuki|UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* Honden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}{{ill|lt=Kakami Ogase-chō|Q131912300|WD=Q131912300}}3-46-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578}}
{{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|WD=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/appeal/bunkazai/000754.html |title=City-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kakamigahara City |accessdate=2013-04-25}} :* Muku tree at Murakuni Shrine Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|Q6810415|WD=Q6810415|lt=Kakamigahara Line}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Ogase Station|Q7079843|WD=Q7079843}}, about 30 minutes on foot. * There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/appeal/bunkazai/000754.html |title=City-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kakamigahara City |accessdate=2013-04-25}} :* Muku tree at Murakuni Shrine from haiden 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-拝殿内部murakuniza034.jpg|Inside the haiden === Location === * 3-46-1 Kakami Ogase-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|Q6810415|WD=Q6810415|lt=Kakamigahara Line}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Ogase Station|Q7079843|WD=Q7079843}}, about 30 minutes on foot. * {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316}} Inaba Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Municipal Kakami Elementary School|Q11412331|WD=Q11412331|lt=Kakami-shō Nishi}}" bus stop, about 20 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Teru |title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Related items === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922}} * {{ill|lt=Kasami former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|WD=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{commons category|Category:Murakuni-jinja}} [[JA:村国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523055}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Murakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza028.jpg|270px]]
Honden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|lt=Kakami Ogase-chō|Q131912300|WD=Q131912300|1=Kakami Ogase-chō}}3-46-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}
{{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|WD=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district|1=Kakami}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460|1=900}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592|1=Jōhō}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/appeal/bunkazai/000754.html |title=City-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kakamigahara City |accessdate=2013-04-25}} :* Muku tree at Murakuni Shrine {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|WD=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district|1=Kakami}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460|1=900}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592|1=Jōhō}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= {{commons category|Category:Murakuni-jinja}} [[JA:村国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523055}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Murakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza028.jpg|270px]]
Honden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|lt=Kakami Ogase-chō|Q131912300|WD=Q131912300|1=Kakami Ogase-chō}}3-46-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}
{{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|WD=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district|1=Kakami}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460|1=900}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592|1=Jōhō}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/appeal/bunkazai/000754.html |title=City-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kakamigahara City |accessdate=2013-04-25}} :* Muku tree at Murakuni Shrine otabisho : === Reference images === 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-村国神社と村国座murakuniza006.jpg|Murakuni Shrine and Murakuniza 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza029.jpg|Honden 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-拝殿から本殿を望むmurakuniza027.jpg|View of honden from haiden 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-拝殿内部murakuniza034.jpg|Inside the haiden === Location === * 3-46-1 Kakami Ogase-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Station|Q7079843|WD=Q7079843|1=Ogase Station}}, about 30 minutes on foot. * {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Inaba Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Municipal Kakami Elementary School|Q11412331|WD=Q11412331|lt=Kakami-shō Nishi|1=Kakami-shō Nishi}}" bus stop, about 20 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons category|Category:Murakuni-jinja}} [[JA:村国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523055}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Murakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza028.jpg|270px]]
Honden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|lt=Kakami Ogase-chō|Q131912300|WD=Q131912300|1=Kakami Ogase-chō}}3-46-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}
{{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|WD=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district|1=Kakami}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460|1=900}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592|1=Jōhō}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/appeal/bunkazai/000754.html |title=City-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kakamigahara City |accessdate=2013-04-25}} :* Muku tree at Murakuni Shrine otabisho : === Reference images === 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-村国神社と村国座murakuniza006.jpg|Murakuni Shrine and Murakuniza 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza029.jpg|Honden 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-拝殿から本殿を望むmurakuniza027.jpg|View of honden from haiden 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-拝殿内部murakuniza034.jpg|Inside the haiden === Location === * 3-46-1 Kakami Ogase-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|Q6810415|WD=Q6810415|lt=Kakamigahara Line|1=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Ogase Station|Q7079843|WD=Q7079843|1=Ogase Station}}, about 30 minutes on foot. * {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Inaba Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Municipal Kakami Elementary School|Q11412331|WD=Q11412331|lt=Kakami-shō Nishi|1=Kakami-shō Nishi}}" bus stop, about 20 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Teru |title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Related items === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Kasami Shrine|Q11398477|WD=Q11398477|1=Kasami Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|Q11523053|WD=Q11523053|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11488771|WD=Q11488771|1=Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|WD=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district|1=Kakami}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460|1=900}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592|1=Jōhō}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/appeal/bunkazai/000754.html |title=City-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kakamigahara City |accessdate=2013-04-25}} :* Muku tree at Murakuni Shrine otabisho : === Reference images === 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-村国神社と村国座murakuniza006.jpg|Murakuni Shrine and Murakuniza 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza029.jpg|Honden 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-拝殿から本殿を望むmurakuniza027.jpg|View of honden from haiden 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-拝殿内部murakuniza034.jpg|Inside the haiden === Location === * 3-46-1 Kakami Ogase-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|Q6810415|WD=Q6810415|lt=Kakamigahara Line|1=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line}}, get off at {{ill|lt=Ogase Station|Q7079843|WD=Q7079843|1=Ogase Station}}, about 30 minutes on foot. * {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Inaba Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Municipal Kakami Elementary School|Q11412331|WD=Q11412331|lt=Kakami-shō Nishi|1=Kakami-shō Nishi}}" bus stop, about 20 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Teru |title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Related items === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Kasami Shrine|Q11398477|WD=Q11398477|1=Kasami Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|Q11523053|WD=Q11523053|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11488771|WD=Q11488771|1=Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated {{commons category|Category:Murakuni-jinja}} [[JA:村国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523055}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Murakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza028.jpg|270px]]
Honden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|lt=Kakami Ogase-chō|Q131912300|WD=Q131912300|1=Kakami Ogase-chō}}3-46-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}
{{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|WD=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district|1=Kakami}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460|1=900}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592|1=Jōhō}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/appeal/bunkazai/000754.html |title=City-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kakamigahara City |accessdate=2013-04-25}} :* Muku tree at Murakuni Shrine from haiden 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-拝殿内部murakuniza034.jpg|Inside the haiden === Location === * 3-46-1 Kakami Ogase-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya "{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Municipal Kakami Elementary School|Q11412331|WD=Q11412331|lt=Kakami-shō Nishi|1=Kakami-shō Nishi}}" bus stop, about 20 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons category|Category:Murakuni-jinja}} [[JA:村国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523055}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Murakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza028.jpg|270px]]
Honden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|lt=Kakami Ogase-chō|Q131912300|WD=Q131912300|1=Kakami Ogase-chō}}3-46-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}
{{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|WD=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district|1=Kakami}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460|1=900}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592|1=Jōhō}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|WD=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district|1=Kakami}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|WD=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|WD=Q11523052|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|WD=Q23460|1=900}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|WD=Q1193592|1=Jōhō}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|WD=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|WD=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{commons category|Category:Murakuni-jinja}} [[JA:村国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523055}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Murakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza028.jpg|270px]]
Honden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|lt=Kakami Ogase-chō|Q131912300|qid=Q131912300|1=Kakami Ogase-chō}}3-46-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|qid=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|qid=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}
{{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|qid=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
Kinpeisha | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|qid=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=Second Saturday and Sunday of October | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Murakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:村国神社 御旅所.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Murakuni no Oyoru burial site]] '''Murakuni Shrine''' (むらくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Kakami area ({{ill|lt=Kakami Village|Q11412364|qid=Q11412364|lt=Kakami district|1=Kakami}}) in Kakamigahara City. It is one of the five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} in Kakamigahara. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two seats of Murakuni Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigōri|Q11412369|qid=Q11412369|1=Kakami district}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (now Kakamigahara). There are two Murakuni Shrines in Kakami Ogase-chō 3-chōme, and the other Murakuni Shrine (3-85) is only about 150 meters away in a straight line (about 50 meters from the approach entrance). The shrine at 85 is said to be the site of the grave of {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|qid=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}, and is now an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|qid=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}. In the precinct next to the haiden stands the playhouse {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|qid=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} ('''Kakami no Butai'''), designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|qid=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/kakami.html |title=Kakami no Butai |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2015-06-06}}. === Outline === It is one of the five Kinpeisha in Kakamigahara City. It is said to have been founded in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|qid=Q210688|1=Asuka period}} by the {{ill|lt=Murakuni clan|Q11523054|qid=Q11523054|1=Murakuni Clan}} with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} and {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|qid=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} as deities. Later, {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Shigamaro|Q11523052|qid=Q11523052|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro}} jointly enshrined {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|qid=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}}. Around {{ill|lt=900|Q23460|qid=Q23460|1=900}} ({{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1193592|qid=Q1193592|1=Jōhō}} 3), Hakusan Gongen was jointly enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|qid=Q7773|1=1877}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10), {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|qid=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} was completed as the venue for the shrine's dedicated plays ({{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|qid=Q199701|1=Kabuki}}), and in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|qid=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) its opening performance (kokerawotoshi) was held. On September 25, {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|qid=Q2477|1=1973}} (Shōwa 48), it was designated by the Gifu Prefectural Shrine Association as a prefectural head-attendance designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation Kin No. 72{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=77}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|qid=Q6080112|lt=Miko Ishikoridome-no-Uba-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|qid=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni no Oyoru|Q6937264|qid=Q6937264|1=Murakuni Oyori}} === Precinct shrine === * Akiba Shrine === Annual festival === Every year on the second Saturday and Sunday of October, dedicated kabuki is performed as the annual festival of Murakuni Shrine. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=jikai kabuki|1=UNKNOWN}} centered on children. === Cultural properties === ; Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) :* {{ill|lt=Murakuniza|Q11523051|qid=Q11523051|1=Murakuni-za}} : ; Kakamigahara City designated natural monuments :* Murakuni Shrine grove{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/appeal/bunkazai/000754.html |title=City-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kakamigahara City |accessdate=2013-04-25}} :* Muku tree at Murakuni Shrine otabisho : === Reference images === 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-村国神社と村国座murakuniza006.jpg|Murakuni Shrine and Murakuniza 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-本殿murakuniza029.jpg|Honden 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-拝殿から本殿を望むmurakuniza027.jpg|View of honden from haiden 村国神社 (各務ヶ原市)-拝殿内部murakuniza034.jpg|Inside the haiden === Location === * 3-46-1 Kakami Ogase-chō, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Station|Q7079843|qid=Q7079843|1=Ogase Station}}, about 30 minutes on foot. * {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Fureai Bus|Q11412316|qid=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Inaba Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Municipal Kakami Elementary School|Q11412331|qid=Q11412331|lt=Kakami-shō Nishi|1=Kakami-shō Nishi}}" bus stop, about 20 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
